body.title: Reply-obligation forking — commit-to-look phrases adopt tracked goals with completion→reply pipeline
body.result: 59a7184c — bouquet-to-TWM for commit-to-look phrases. goal_adopt extended w/ origin context (thread_id, turn_id, question, awaiting_reply); submit_background propagates goal_id through completion item; fork_bg dispatch adopts goal first when awaiting_reply set; completion drain pushes bouquet (origin_question 0.92 + active_goal refresh 0.93 + pending_reply marker 0.90) instead of composing reply. New habit PROC_REPLY_OBLIGATION_LOOK with narrow commit-to-look triggers, conversation_eligible, intent-gated. Forensic log at logs/reply_obligations.log. 6 unit tests + 1966 regression tests pass. CLAUDE.md persona section added (biomimicry engineer) — meta-fix that produced the better plan.
body.description:
Diagnosed from /home/akien/.TheIgors/akien/outbox/20260412.IkienIgorChatWThreadProblems.txt — Igor lost track three times in one conversation. Akien had to prompt 'what did you find?' because nothing pulled Igor back to deliver answers he committed to.

ROOT CAUSE: When Igor says 'let me look at the ticket list' or 'thinking about that,' he forks the work but not a tracked reply-obligation. No goal hangs in the salience field demanding completion, so when the work resolves nothing composes a reply into the originating thread.

T-onit-rebuild already added goal_adopt + TWM-pushed GOAL memory (salience=0.85 ttl=7200s). T-on-it-fork added fork_bg:true. But two gaps remain:

1) TRIGGER SURFACE TOO NARROW. PROC_ON_IT_TWM_PUSH only matches 'On it'-class task-assignment phrases. Igor said 'let me look at my ticket list and the relevant facia memories' — that didn't match. Need broadened triggers covering: 'let me look/check/find/see,' 'I'll look/check,' 'thinking about that,' 'let me find out,' 'I should check.' All conversational commitments to look/check/think should adopt a reply-obligation goal.

2) NO COMPLETION→REPLY PIPELINE. Goals get adopted but nothing wires 'underlying task resolved → compose reply into originating thread.' record_completion() hookpoint exists in anticipation.py (per T-if-fork notes). Wire it to: scan active reply-obligation goals, find any whose dependent task just resolved, fire a habit that composes a reply into the originating thread/turn.

3) GOALS CARRY ORIGINATING CONTEXT. Reply-obligation goals must record (thread_id, turn_id, original_question, what_I_promised_to_check) so composition has the context to land the reply correctly.

FALLOUT BENEFIT: An unfulfilled reply-obligation goal becomes the dominant attractor (salience 0.85). 'What did you find?' two turns later routes naturally via active goal context — no separate intent-routing fix needed. This subsumes the conversational-primacy bug.

INERTIA: HIGH-MEDIUM. Touches habit dispatch (main.py), goal system (ops.py / models.py GOAL type), composition pipeline (anticipation.py record_completion), possibly intent classifier. CC-owned per CLAUDE.md HIGH-inertia rule.

TESTS REQUIRED:
- Habit fires on broadened trigger phrases ('let me look at,' 'thinking about that,' 'I'll check')
- goal_adopt records originating thread/turn/question metadata
- record_completion fires reply-composition habit when task resolves with active reply-obligation
- end-to-end: simulate 'let me look at X' → background task completes → reply composed and emitted into originating thread without external prompt
- regression: 'On it' / T-onit-rebuild path still works

FORENSIC LOGGING: Log every reply-obligation adoption (trigger phrase, goal id, thread/turn ids), every completion fire (goal id, elapsed since adoption, dependent task id), every composition (goal id → reply text). Lets us verify the loop closes and measure how long obligations stay open.

SCOPE BOUNDARY: This ticket only handles spoken commitments to look/check/think. Does NOT handle: (a) implicit obligations from questions Igor doesn't acknowledge, (b) multi-step plans where Igor commits to several things, (c) reply-obligations that span Igor restarts. Those are separate tickets if needed.

DEPENDENCY: None blocking. Built on T-onit-rebuild (goal_adopt), T-on-it-fork (fork_bg flag), record_completion hookpoint.
